tweets.text:
My brother @Spicy_Rich just had every single one of his Rabby wallets drained.

Really freaking sucks.

Lost the majority of what he has invested in crypto one month after having his first son.

Trying to track down where the funds went and figure out ways to help him, but probably won't be as active today trying to figure all of this out.

Just hate seeing someone you care about so much who only does things the right way and actually cares about the communities and projects he invests in have this happen to them.

tweets.text:
cash-cat:native 

As long as the Bitcoin has a bit of strength, I'd be shocked if this the last leg up we see for Robinhood Chain's largest meme.

They're still the king of retail traders and the epicenter of the WSB / meme movement from 2021, and they've yet to list any of their native memecoins on their main app yet.

And it's crazy to think that they won't do it, and that they won't do anything else to promote projects from within their own ecosystem.

Does this guarantee that they have the same type of success as ETH L2s like BASE?

No - but I do think that it's a pretty reasonable bet that $100M is nowhere near close to what the retail meme trading app will see for its highest native memecoin.

Also think it's bullish we're starting to see movement outside of CASHCAT within the eco. Multiple runners attracts more liquidity. So seeing both TENDIES and CASHCAT do well drives more demand to accelerate the adoption timeline.

Point being - still early.

thread_tweets.text:
One other note - my current thesis for Bitcoin / the broader crypto market is pump until sometime in mid August before we see a bit of distribution at a new local top and one last leg lower that creates a true bottom for this bear market in Sept/early Oct.

So - that opens a window of about another month that high momentum plays like Robinhood Chain assets have the opportunity to run.

Also lines up with when I plan on taking some profits in my positions because I believe there will be an opportunity to stack lower.

I know it sounds like a month is a very short window, but if you've been around crypto for long enough - a LOT can happen within a month.

So even though my ~general~ bias is still that we haven't seen the bottom of the bear market yet, that doesn't mean there aren't windows of opportunity to be bullish within certain sectors and have a bit of fun.

tweets.text: Pending home sales fell in June on both a monthly and annual basis, driven by a 10-month high in mortgage rates and record-high home prices. Contracts sank 5.4% compared with May and fell 0.3% YoY. The MoM pullback was nationwide, while YoY sales fell only in the South and West.
